ingredients.name: Season Sardine Fillets - no salt added
Safe Catch Salmon - no salt added
Safe Catch Tuna - no salt added
Vegetable stock (Better Than Bouillon low sodium)
Go Macro Mini - Coco + Almond Butter + Choco Chips
Go Macro Mini - PB + Choco Chip

recipes.name: Cauliflower Salad with Dates and Pistachios
recipes.instructions:
1. Heat oven to 450°F and coat a large baking sheet well with olive oil, then sprinkle the pan with 1/4 tsp salt and black pepper. Quarter your cauliflower, cut out the core, then tear the quarters into bite-sized chunks. Dice the core into smaller pieces. Place on prepared baking sheet and toss to coat in the oil, salt, and pepper.
2. Lop the greens off *half* your scallions and slice them thinly, setting them aside. Cut remaining whole and white half of scallions 1-to-2-inch segments. Clear a little space at the end of your cauliflower baking sheet and add the scallion segments to it. Season the pan with additional salt and pepper.
3. Roast for 10 minutes then remove tray from oven (leave oven on) and use tongs to remove just the charred scallion segments, placing them in a large bowl. Turn cauliflower pieces over for even browning and return it to the oven for another 5 to 10 minutes, until cauliflower is deeply browned in many spots. Don’t fear the char — it will taste good, not burnt.
4. Meanwhile, roughly chop the pistachios. Pit your dates, if needed, and roughly chop. Make a vinaigrette with olive oil, the juice of one lemon, plus enough pepper to season it very well. Whisk (or blend, if you are as obsessed with your electric frother as I am). We want a sharp lemon dressing here, it will get milder as it coats the warm ingrednets, but if it’s definitely too sharp for your taste, blend 1 additional tablespoon olive oil.
5. When cauliflower is done, add to bowl with charred scallions, along with dates, pistachios, and reserved scallion greens. Toss with dressing to taste (I use almost all of it), seasoning further if needed. Salad is good right away but it also keeps well at room temperature for a few hours. [Leftovers are great warmed in the microwave for just 10 to 20 seconds, just to take the coldest chill off it it
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

### Top nutrient contributors

**Calories:** Dates (328 kcal) and pistachios (320 kcal) together account for ~68% of total calories. Olive oil adds another 120 kcal.

**Protein:** Pistachios (12g) and cauliflower (11.2g) are the two meaningful sources. Scallions add ~2.3g.

**Sugar:** Almost entirely from Medjool dates. 8 dates drive the 23.8g/serving figure — reducing to 5 dates is the primary optimization.

**Sodium:** The 1 tsp salt is the dominant driver at ~624mg/serving. Halving it brings this into a more reasonable range for a side dish.

**Fiber:** Strong at 8.45g/serving across cauliflower, dates, and pistachios. Well-preserved in the optimized version.

---

### Proposed substitutions

**1. Dates: 8 → 5 Medjool dates (existing ingredient ✓)**
- **Why:** Keeps the dish's defining character — caramel chew against roasted char — while meaningfully reducing sugar. 5 dates still gets you a date in every bite across 4 servings.
- **Flavor/texture:** Negligible. The lemon dressing and charred scallions will read as slightly more prominent without the sweetness competing as hard, which is arguably an improvement.

**2. Salt: 1 tsp → ½ tsp (existing ingredient ✓)**
- **Why:** At 624mg/serving for a side, sodium is the easiest lever to pull. The lemon vinaigrette and roasting process do most of the seasoning work. The recipe already invites final seasoning adjustments.
- **Flavor/texture:** Minimal impact. The dressing is the primary seasoning vehicle here.

No stubs. Both changes use existing database ingredients.

**Fat assessment:** 10.8g/serving with only 1.3g saturated — left unchanged. Almost entirely unsaturated fat from olive oil and pistachios. Reducing olive oil would hurt texture and palatability more than the numbers would justify.

---

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 239 kcal | ~209 kcal | −30 kcal |
| Fat | 10.8g | 10.8g | — |
| Saturated Fat | 1.3g | 1.3g | — |
| Sodium | 624mg | ~312mg | −312mg |
| Carbs | 35.7g | ~28g | −7.7g |
| Sugar | 23.8g | ~11g | −12.8g |
| Protein | 6.4g | 6.4g | — |
| Fiber | 8.5g | ~7.8g | −0.7g |

recipes.name: Overnight Oats Blueberry - Jen
recipes.instructions:
1, Add frozen blueberries + 1 Tbsp lemon juice to a saucepan and cook down. Let cool.
2. Put all overnight ingredients into a bowl.
3. If desired, separate into containers and then top with blueberry mixture, lemon zest, and almonds.

recipes.name: Tuna "Toast" w/Egg
recipes.instructions:
1. Blend tuna, 1 egg, nutritional yeast
2. Cook into 2 "toast" patties
3. Top with avocado, fried eggs, salt

recipes.name: PB Protein Balls
recipes.instructions: for PB powder -- mix with 48g of water to form paste before mixing into the rest of the mixture

recipes.name: Black Bean Avocado Brownies
recipes.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350F. Grease and line 8x8 pan.
2. Place all ingredients besides chocolate chips into a food processor and blend til smooth. This batter needs to be thick.
3. Add in chocolate chips and fold into batter.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan. Add in extra chips, nuts, or peanut butter swirl (optional)
5. Bake for 25-30min. Let cool before cutting.

recipes.name: Spiced Chickpea Stew with Coconut and Turmeric
recipes.instructions:
1. Heat 1/4 cup oil in a large pot over medium. Add garlic, onion and ginger. Season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally until onion is translucent and starts to brown a little at the edges, 3 to 5 minutes.
2. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons turmeric, 1 teaspoon red-pepper flakes, and the chickpeas, and season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring frequently, so the chickpeas sizzle and fry a bit in the spices and oil, until they’ve started to break down and get a little browned and crisp, 8 to 10 minutes. Remove about a cup of chickpeas and set aside for garnish.
3. Using a wooden spoon or spatula, further crush the remaining chickpeas slightly to release their starchy insides. (This will help thicken the stew.) Add coconut milk and stock, and season with salt and pepper.
4. Bring to a simmer, scraping up any bits that have formed on the bottom of the pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until stew has thickened, 30 to 35 minutes. (Taste a chickpea or two, not just the liquid, to make sure they have simmered long enough to be as delicious as possible.) If after 30 to 35 minutes, you want the stew a bit thicker, keep simmering until you've reached your desired consistency. Determining perfect stew thickness is a personal journey!
5. Add greens and stir, making sure they’re submerged in the liquid. Cook until they wilt and soften, 3 to 7 minutes, depending on what you’re using. (Swiss chard and spinach will wilt and soften much faster than kale or collard greens.) Season again with salt and pepper.
6. Divide among bowls and top with mint, reserved chickpeas, a sprinkle of red-pepper flakes and a good drizzle of olive oil. Serve alongside yogurt and toasted pita if using; dust the yogurt with turmeric if you'd like.

recipes.name: Miso Leeks w/White Beans (and Toast)
recipes.instructions:
1. Prepare the leeks: Place the sliced leeks into a colander and rinse them well, rubbing to loosen any dirt. Rinse them again and drain well. (There is no need to dry them, as the residual water is useful in cooking the leeks.)
2. Heat a large Dutch oven or skillet on medium-high for 1 to 2 minutes. Add 2 tablespoons of olive oil, along with the leeks. Season generously with salt and black pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until the liquid cooks off and leeks start to stick to the pan, 5 to 6 minutes. Stir in the garlic and thyme. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Meanwhile, bring a medium pot of water to a boil over high. Add the eggs and continue to cook over medium-high for 7 minutes. (Make sure you set a timer.) Set up an ice bath. Using a spider ladle or slotted spoon, remove the eggs from the water and immediately add them to the ice bath. Cool for 3 to 4 minutes, then peel and set aside.
4. Make the vinaigrette: In a large bowl, whisk together the miso paste, Dijon mustard, garlic, vinegar and olive oil.
5. Uncover the leeks — they will have reduced into a silky, jammy consistency. Discard the thyme, transfer the leeks to the bowl and stir to coat in the miso vinaigrette.
6. Stir in the white beans and leave to cool for a few minutes. Add about 3/4 of the tarragon or parsley leaves and toss gently to combine.
7. Divide the leeks and beans among bowls and drizzle each with olive oil. Top with a halved jammy egg and finish with the remaining herbs. Serve with toast, if desired.

recipes.name: Roasted Tahini Cauliflower Steak with Lentils
recipes.instructions:
1. Set oven to 425F. Place the lentils, vegetable broth, thyme and rosemary in a sauce pan. Bring to a boil and then set to a simmer. Cook the lentils according to package until the lentils are tender and have absorbed most of the liquid. NOTE - dissolve 12g of bouillion in 3 cups of water
2. Trim the leaves away from your cauliflower and then cut the cauliflower head into 3-4 even slabs.
3. Add the tahini, soy sauce, nutritional yeast, herbs, and garlic to a small bowl and whisk together. When the mixture clumps together, whisk in the water to thin the mixture out.
4. Brush the cauliflower with the mixture on both sides and place on a parchment lined baking sheet.
5. Place the baking tray in an oven and bake for 15 minutes. Then, set oven to high broil and allow to cook for an additional 5-8 minutes or until the edges are golden.
6. Allow to cool for at least 5 mins before serving along with the lentils.

recipes.name: Crispy Salmon w/ Coconut Rice
recipes.instructions:
1. START THE COCONUT RICE:
2. In a fine-mesh strainer, rinse 2 cups long-grain white rice until the water runs clear. Drain well and add to a medium saucepan with a tight-fitting lid.
3. Crack open 1(13.5-ounce) can full-fat coconut milk. Set aside a few tablespoons of the coconut milk in a small bowl. Stir the remaining coconut milk into the saucepan with the rice, ¾ cup water, and a big pinch of salt. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat. As soon as it simmers, reduce the heat to as low as possible, cover with the lid, and set a timer for 16 minutes. When the timer goes off, remove the pan from the heat and keep covered until ready to serve.
4. While the rice cooks, thinly slice 1 English cucumber crosswise (use a mandoline if you have one!). Toss the sliced cucumber with salt to taste in a medium bowl and keep chilled until ready to serve.
5. MAKE THE CRACKLE SAUCE:
6. Chop or coarsely crush ½ cup roasted, salted peanuts. In a large nonstick skillet, combine the chopped peanuts, ¼ cup olive oil, and 3 tablespoons sesame seeds. Set the skillet over medium heat and cook, stirring often, until the peanuts are several shades darker and the sesame seeds are toasted, 6 to 8 minutes.
7. Transfer to a small bowl. While the mixture is still hot, immediately finely grate 2 garlic cloves and 1 (½-inch) piece of fresh ginger right into the oil. Stir in 1½ teaspoons red pepper flakes, 1 teaspoon ground turmeric, 1 teaspoon honey, and the reserved coconut milk and season the sauce with salt. YOU'RE PROBS NOT USING ENOUGH ... SALT (PG 20)
8. COOK THE SALMON:
9. Wipe out the nonstick skillet with a paper towel. Season 4 (6-ounce) skin-on salmon fillets all over with salt. Working in batches if needed, arrange the salmon fillets, skin-side down, in the skillet and set over medium heat. Cook, pressing down on the top of the salmon occasionally to encourage it to make contact with the pan, until the skin is golden and very crisp, 7 to 9 minutes. If the skin's not looking crispy enough, keep cooking! Don't settle for anything less than crisp.
10. Flip the fillets and cook until the salmon is mostly opaque on the sides, with a slight hint of translucence, 1 to 2 minutes longer!
11. SERVE:
12. Fluff the coconut rice with a fork and divide it among shallow bows.
13. Top each bowl with a salmon fillet and a pile of salted cucumbers. Drizzle the crackle sauce over the top.
recipes.mealPrepAnalysis:
## Meal Prep Analysis

### Component-by-component breakdown

**Coconut Rice**
Stores and reheats very well. The fat from the coconut milk keeps it from drying out as much as plain rice. Holds 4–5 days in the fridge. Does not freeze especially well — the texture turns slightly gummy on thaw, but it's acceptable if portioned tightly.

**Crackle Sauce (peanut/sesame/oil infusion)**
The MVP of this recipe for meal prep. Shelf-stable in the fridge for at least a week, arguably two. The fat base keeps everything preserved. Flavor actually deepens over a couple days. Make a double batch.

**Salmon**
The weak link. Crispy skin is a right-now texture — it will soften within an hour of cooking. The fish itself holds fine in the fridge 2–3 days, but you're giving up the defining textural element of this dish. Best approach: batch the rice and sauce, cook salmon fresh or day-of.

**Cucumber**
Do not prep in advance beyond slicing. The salted cucumber will continue weeping liquid and get waterlogged. Slice and salt right before serving — 2 minutes of work.

---

### Reheating instructions

| Component | Method | Temp | Time |
|---|---|---|---|
| Coconut rice | Stovetop with a splash of water or coconut milk, covered | Medium-low | 3–4 min, stirring once |
| Coconut rice (alt) | Microwave with damp paper towel over bowl | High | 1.5–2 min |
| Crackle sauce | Stovetop or microwave | Low / 30 sec bursts | Just warmed through — don't overheat or the garlic turns bitter |
| Salmon (day-old) | Cast iron or nonstick, skin-side down, no lid | Medium | 3–4 min skin-side, 1 min flip — skip microwave entirely |
| Salmon (frozen/thawed) | Same as above after fully thawing overnight in fridge | Medium | Same |

---

### Batch cooking recommendation

Rice and crackle sauce batch well. Salmon is better cooked fresh per serving. A batch of 8 servings (2×) is practical for a week of lunches or dinners for two.

**Scaled quantities — 8 servings (2×)**

| Ingredient | Base (4 srv) | Batch (8 srv) |
|---|---|---|
| Salmon, raw | 750 g | 1500 g |
| White rice (dry) | 2 cups | 4 cups |
| Light coconut milk | 400 g | 800 g |
| Olive oil | ¼ cup | ½ cup |
| Peanuts | ½ cup | 1 cup |
| Sesame seeds | 3 tbsp | 6 tbsp |
| Garlic | 2 cloves | 4 cloves |
| Ginger | 5 g | 10 g |
| Red pepper flakes | 1.5 tsp | 1 tbsp |
| Turmeric | 1 tsp | 2 tsp |
| Honey | 1 tsp | 2 tsp |
| Cucumber | 300 g | 600 g (buy fresh as needed) |

---

### This week vs. freeze for later

**This week (fridge):**
- All the coconut rice (4–5 day window)
- Full batch of crackle sauce
- 2–3 salmon fillets if you want a grab-and-reheat option on busy nights

**Freeze for later:**
- Cooked coconut rice in individual portions — add a little liquid on reheat
- Raw salmon portions individually wrapped — freeze raw, cook fresh on use day
- Crackle sauce: fridge life is long enough that freezing isn't necessary

---

### Day-by-day plan (Sunday prep session)

**Sunday** — Cook full rice batch. Make double crackle sauce. Cook 1–2 salmon fillets fresh for dinner.
**Monday** — Reheat rice. Cook salmon fresh. Slice cucumber to order.
**Tuesday** — Same. Sauce is better today.
**Wednesday** — Reheat rice. Last good day for any fridge salmon; otherwise cook fresh.
**Thursday** — Rice still fine with splash of liquid on reheat. Cook salmon fresh. Last call for rice.
**Friday/Weekend** — Finish remaining or pivot. If salmon was frozen raw, pull Thursday night to thaw.

---

### Storage summary

| Component | Fridge | Freezer |
|---|---|---|
| Coconut rice (cooked) | 4–5 days | 1 month (texture degrades slightly) |
| Crackle sauce | 7–10 days | Not necessary |
| Salmon (cooked) | 2–3 days | Not recommended |
| Salmon (raw, portioned) | 1–2 days | 2–3 months |
| Cucumber (sliced/salted) | Same day only | Do not freeze |

recipes.name: Sesame Miso Lentils
recipes.instructions:
1. Add lentils and vegetable broth to a sauce pan and bring to a boil. Reduce to a simmer and allow lentils to cook with the lid off for about 15 minutes. You should see that the lentils mostly absorb all the liquid. Test the lentils to make sure they are cooked through and if there’s extra cooking liquid, drain it off with the help of a colander or strainer. Allow to sit to slightly cool.
2. While lentils are cooking, prepare the dressing. To a medium mixing bowl, add tahini, chili sauce, garlic powder, soy sauce, mustard, sesame oil, miso, lemon juice and water. Whisk well to combine then set aside.
3. Add lentils to the bowl with the tahini sauce and add in onions and cilantro. Give everything a good mix and then serve.

recipes.name: Curried Red Lentils
recipes.instructions:
1. To a large saucepan on medium heat, add the olive oil. Gently cook the onions for 5 minutes then add the ginger, black pepper, curry powder, fennel and cook for 1 minute before adding the lentils.
2. Stir the lentils through the spices, add the tomato puree, spinach and boiling water. Bring to a simmer and cover with a lid for 10 to 12 minutes, until the lentils have cooked.
3. Uncover and add the peas. Cook for another 2 minutes.
4. Take off the heat and serve with the yoghurt.

recipes.name: One-pan Indian Style Fish and Chickpeas
recipes.instructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large, heavy-bottomed saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the onion, ginger, and garlic with a sprinkle of salt and cook for 3-4 minutes, until softened.
2. Add the curry powder, turmeric, and cumin and stir to coat.
3. Add the tomatoes and cook for 3-4 minutes, until softening.
4. Add the chickpeas and coconut milk, bring to a simmer and cook for 3-4 minutes, until slightly thickened.
5. Add the spinach and cook for 2-3 minutes, just until wilted. Then stir through the tahini.
6. Nestle the fish into the simmering sauce. Spoon some liquid over the top to ensure it is submerged in liquid. Cover, and cook for 7-8 minutes, until the fish is firm to the touch.
7. Serve with fresh coriander and lime.

recipes.name: Turmeric Waffles
recipes.instructions:
1. I. Preheat your waffle iron according to the manufacturer's instructions.
2. 2. In the bowl of a stand mixer fitted with the paddle attachment, combine the coconut milk, melted coconut oil, maple syrup, vinegar, vanilla, almond flour, turmeric, baking soda, flax meal, salt, black pepper, and eggs and beat on medium speed until completely combined.
3. Carefully brush the waffle iron with coconut oil or cooking spray. Pour batter into waffle iron. Cook and transfer to warm plate.

recipes.name: Black Bean Cherry Avocado Brownies
recipes.instructions:
1. Cook down cherries w/ lemon juice. 
2. Preheat oven to 350F. Grease and line 8x8 pan.
3. Place all ingredients besides chocolate chips and cherries into a food processor and blend til smooth. This batter needs to be thick.
4. Add in chocolate chips and cherries and fold into batter.
5. Pour batter into prepared pan. Add in extra chips, nuts, or peanut butter swirl (optional)
6. Bake for 25-30min. Let cool before cutting.

recipes.name: Almond Croissant Bars
recipes.instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (180°C). Line an 8-inch baking pan with parchment paper. Lightly oil with cooking oil spray. Set aside.
2. In a mixing bowl, stir almond flour, quick oats, almond milk, maple syrup, and almond extract until smooth. It won't be too thick, a bit liquid, that's normal.
3. Pour into the prepared pan. Rest 10 minutes to give time to the fibers in oats to absorb the liquid. Meanwhile, make the topping.
4. In a small bowl, stir all the almond paste ingredients until they form a thick paste that resembles marzipan. If too dry, add some almond milk. It should be thick like a paste but not crumbly.
5. Drop dollops of the paste all over the pan and press to spread roughly all over the pan and create a second layer on top. It won't cover all the first batter layer, that's fine.
6. Sprinkle the sliced almonds all over the top and press a little so they stick to the batter.
7. Bake the bars on the center rack of the oven for 40 minutes at 350°F (180°C) until the top is set, golden, and a toothpick inserted in the center of the bars comes out clean.
8. Let the bars cool down for 10 minutes in the pan at room temperature, then cool down for 1 hour on a cooling rack before slicing.
9. Slice into 9 bars and serve as a breakfast with a drizzle of maple syrup and a dollop of yogurt. Dust with powdered sugar just before serving.

recipes.name: One-Pot Pantry Orzo With Chickpeas
recipes.instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven and preheat to 350°. Heat ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy ovenproof pot over medium. Add 12 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, and cook, stirring often, until softened and fragrant, 2–3 minutes. Add one 14-oz. jar pitted green olives, drained, coarsely chopped (about 1 cup), one 3.5-oz. jar capers, rinsed (about ½ cup), and ¼ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes and cook, stirring occasionally, just until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
2. Add 12 oz. orzo and one 6-oz. can tomato paste to pot and cook, stirring often, until tomato paste coats orzo and is darkened in color, about 4 minutes. Add one 15.5-oz. can chickpeas, rinsed, 1 tsp. dried oregano, 1 tsp. sugar, 1½ tsp. Diamond Crystal or ¾ tsp. Morton kosher salt, and 4⅓ cups water, stirring to scrape up any browned bits stuck to bottom of pot.
3. Cover pot, transfer to oven, and bake until orzo is tender and about 80% of liquid has been absorbed, 30–35 minutes (having some liquid on top is okay as the orzo will continue to absorb it). Remove pot from oven, remove lid, and stir in 2 oz. Parmesan, finely grated (about 1 cup). Taste orzo and season with more salt if needed and freshly ground black pepper.
4. To serve, divide orzo evenly among shallow bowls. Top each bowl of orzo with more Parmesan if desired, then season with a little more black pepper.

recipes.name: Almond Croissant Bars (Optimized)
recipes.instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (180°C). Line an 8-inch baking pan with parchment paper. Lightly oil with cooking oil spray. Set aside.

2. **Base layer:** In a mixing bowl, stir almond flour, oat flour, oats, unsweetened almond milk, maple syrup, and almond extract until smooth. The batter will be fairly liquid — that's normal.

3. Pour into the prepared pan. Rest 10 minutes to allow the oat fibers to absorb the liquid. Meanwhile, make the topping.

4. **Topping/paste layer:** In a small bowl, combine almond flour, oat flour, softened butter, applesauce, maple syrup, and almond extract. Stir until a thick paste forms. It will be slightly more dense than traditional marzipan. If too dry, add almond milk 1 tsp at a time. It should hold together without being crumbly.

5. Drop dollops of the paste all over the pan and press to spread roughly into a second layer on top. It won't cover the entire base layer — that's fine.

6. Sprinkle the slivered almonds over the top and press lightly so they adhere.

7. Bake on the center rack for 40 minutes at 350°F (180°C) until the top is set and golden, and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

8. Let cool 10 minutes in the pan, then transfer to a cooling rack for at least 1 hour before slicing. The bars need the full cool time to set properly — the applesauce in the paste makes them slightly softer than the original when warm.

9. Slice into 9 bars. Serve with a dollop of yogurt if desired. No powdered sugar dusting needed.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

Optimized from Almond Croissant Bars (recipe #29). Goal: reduce fat and sugar while preserving protein, fiber, and the almond croissant character. The increased almond extract is the key move — it reads as rich and sweet without adding calories.

---

## Base layer

| Ingredient | Original | Optimized | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Almond flour | 1 cup | 3/4 cup | Reduces fat; oat flour fills the volume |
| Oat flour | — | 1/4 cup | Lower fat, blends seamlessly in wet batter. Negligible flavor and texture impact. |
| Maple syrup | 1/4 cup | 3 tbsp | Modest sugar reduction; oats and almond extract carry the sweetness |
| Cashew milk | 1 1/3 cup | 1 1/3 cup unsweetened almond milk | Lower fat and sugar, no detectable flavor difference |

## Topping / paste layer

| Ingredient | Original | Optimized | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Butter | 4 tbsp | 2 tbsp | Cuts fat and saturated fat significantly |
| Unsweetened applesauce | — | 2 tbsp | Replaces half the butter; keeps paste moist, adds subtle natural sweetness. Bars set slightly softer — allow full cool before cutting. |
| Almond flour | 1 cup | 1/2 cup | Largest single fat reduction in the recipe |
| Oat flour | — | 1/2 cup | Replaces volume; paste will be slightly more dense and bread-like, less marzipan-tender |
| Maple syrup | 3 tbsp | 2 tbsp | Further sugar reduction. Add 1 tbsp almond milk if paste feels too dry. |
| Almond extract | 1 tsp | 1.5 tsp | **Key move.** Increased extract compensates perceptually for less fat and sugar. Almond extract reads as rich and sweet without adding calories. Do not skip this increase. |

## Topping

| Ingredient | Original | Optimized | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Slivered almonds | 3/4 cup | 1/2 cup | Reduces fat and calories while maintaining crunch and visual coverage |
| Powdered sugar | 1/4 tsp garnish | Omitted | Negligible nutrition but unnecessary |

---

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 604 kcal | 503 kcal | −101 |
| Total fat | 31.4 g | 21.2 g | −10.2 g |
| Saturated fat | 5.3 g | 3.4 g | −1.9 g |
| Carbs | 62.6 g | 62.9 g | ≈ same |
| Sugar | 13 g | 9.3 g | −3.7 g |
| Protein | 20.6 g | 17.2 g | −3.4 g |
| Fiber | 13.1 g | 11.6 g | −1.5 g |

---

## Texture and flavor notes

- The paste layer will be slightly more dense and less marzipan-tender than the original. This is expected.
- Applesauce makes the bars softer when warm — allow the full 1-hour cool before slicing.
- Almond flavor is fully preserved due to the extract increase. The bars do not taste "diet."
- The base is slightly more oat-forward, which complements the less-rich topping layer.

ingredients.notes: increased to compensate for less fat and sugar

recipes.name: Spiced Chickpea Stew with Coconut and Turmeric (Optimized)
recipes.instructions:
1. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large pot over medium. Add garlic, onion and ginger. Season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring occasionally until onion is translucent and starts to brown a little at the edges, 3 to 5 minutes.
2. Add 1 1/2 teaspoons turmeric, 1 teaspoon red-pepper flakes, and the chickpeas, and season with pepper. Cook, stirring frequently, so the chickpeas sizzle and fry a bit in the spices and oil, until they've started to break down and get a little browned and crisp, 8 to 10 minutes. Remove about a cup of chickpeas and set aside for garnish.
3. Using a wooden spoon or spatula, further crush the remaining chickpeas slightly to release their starchy insides. (This will help thicken the stew.) Add coconut milk, 1 cup water, and stock, and season with pepper.
4. Bring to a simmer, scraping up any bits that have formed on the bottom of the pot. Cook, stirring occasionally, until stew has thickened, 30 to 35 minutes. If after 30 to 35 minutes you want the stew a bit thicker, keep simmering until you've reached your desired consistency.
5. Add greens and stir, making sure they're submerged in the liquid. Cook until they wilt and soften, 3 to 7 minutes. Season again with pepper.
6. Divide among bowls and top with mint, reserved chickpeas, and a sprinkle of red-pepper flakes. Serve alongside yogurt (3 tbsp per serving) swirled in or on the side; dust the yogurt with turmeric if you'd like.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

Optimized from: Spiced Chickpea Stew with Coconut and Turmeric (id:14)

## Goals
Reduce sodium, fat, and saturated fat. Maintain or increase protein and fiber. Preserve flavor integrity.

## Changes Made

| Ingredient | Original | Optimized | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Olive oil | 1/4 cup (4 tbsp) | 2 tbsp | Halved. Still enough to properly sauté and blister chickpeas. Garnish drizzle removed. |
| Salt | 2 tsp | 1 tsp | Largest single sodium lever (~1,163 mg/serving reduction). Finish seasoning at the table if needed. |
| Better Than Bouillon | 12g | 6g + 1 cup water added | Reduces sodium while maintaining volume. Slight reduction in savory depth offset by spices. |
| Coconut Milk - Light | 3.3 cups | 2 cups (+ 1 cup water) | Reduces fat, sat fat, and sugar. Stew stays thick via chickpea starch. Coconut flavor lightens slightly, spices come forward. |
| Garbanzo beans / Chickpeas | 3.5 cups | 4 cups | Boosts protein (+~4.6g/serving) and fiber (+~1.4g/serving). More textural presence. |
| Fage Lactose Free Yogurt | 1/2 cup | 3/4 cup | Served alongside/swirled in. Adds ~5.6g protein/serving, creaminess, and tang. |

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| Nutrient | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 560 kcal | ~490 kcal | −70 |
| Fat | 32.3 g | ~21 g | −11 |
| Saturated Fat | 14.0 g | ~9 g | −5 |
| Sodium | 2,117 mg | ~857 mg | −1,260 |
| Carbs | 43.8 g | ~46 g | +2 |
| Sugar | 9.4 g | ~7.5 g | −2 |
| Protein | 15.5 g | ~25 g | +9.5 |
| Fiber | 12.2 g | ~13.6 g | +1.4 |

## Notes
- All substitutions use existing Good Measure database ingredients — no stubs created.
- Flavor profile is preserved. The chickpeas become more prominent as the coconut lightens, which suits the dish well.
- Sodium reduction is significant; season to taste at the table for individual preference.
recipes.mealPrepAnalysis:
## Meal Prep Notes

### Component-by-component breakdown

**Stew base (chickpeas + broth + coconut milk + aromatics)**
Meal preps exceptionally well. Flavors deepen and improve over 24–48 hours as the spices meld. Chickpeas continue to absorb liquid in storage, thickening the stew further — add a splash of water when reheating if too thick. Holds 4–5 days in the fridge without quality loss. Freezes beautifully for up to 3 months.

**Chard**
Already wilted into the stew, so it just gets softer over time — no significant degradation. Store right in the stew. For more textural contrast later in the week, stir in a handful of fresh chard when reheating a portion.

**Garnish chickpeas (reserved crispy ones)**
Will lose crispiness within a few hours of storage. Store separately in a small container or paper towel-lined bag in the fridge. Re-crisp in a dry skillet or toaster oven before serving. Do not freeze — texture won't survive.

**Mint**
Do not store with the stew. Keep fresh mint whole in a small glass of water in the fridge, loosely covered. Lasts 4–5 days. Chop fresh per serving.

**Yogurt**
Store separately, always. Never mix into the stew for storage — the acid and protein will curdle and separate when reheated. Spoon alongside or swirl in at serving time.

---

### Reheating instructions

| Component | Method | Temp / Time |
|---|---|---|
| Stew base | Stovetop (preferred) | Medium-low, 5–7 min, stirring occasionally. Add splash of water if too thick. |
| Stew base | Microwave | 2–3 min, covered loosely, stir halfway through |
| Garnish chickpeas | Dry skillet or toaster oven | Medium-high, 3–4 min until re-crisped |
| Chard (in stew) | Reheats with the stew | No separate action needed |
| Mint | No reheating | Add fresh at serving |
| Yogurt | No reheating | Serve cold alongside |

---

### Batch cooking recommendation

A double batch (8 servings) is the sweet spot — fills a standard Dutch oven, simmer time stays the same, effort per serving drops significantly.

**Scaled quantities — 8 servings (2×)**

| Ingredient | Base (4 srv) | Batch (2×) |
|---|---|---|
| Olive oil | 2 tbsp | ¼ cup |
| Garlic | 4 cloves | 8 cloves |
| Onion | 1 | 2 |
| Ginger | 20g | 40g |
| Salt | 1 tsp | 2 tsp |
| Turmeric | 1½ tsp | 1 tbsp |
| Red pepper flakes | 1 tsp | 2 tsp |
| Chickpeas | 4 cups | 8 cups |
| Pepper | 2 tsp | 4 tsp |
| Coconut milk (light) | 2 cups | 4 cups |
| Better Than Bouillon | 6g | 12g |
| Water | 1 cup | 2 cups |
| Chard | 400g | 800g |
| Mint | 25g | 50g |
| Fage yogurt | ¾ cup | 1½ cups |

Note: At double batch, add chard in two batches — the pot will be full and it needs room to wilt down before adding the second half.

---

### This week vs. freeze for later

**Eat this week (fridge):** 3–4 servings. Quality peaks around Tuesday and Wednesday.

**Freeze now:** If making a double batch, freeze remaining 4–5 servings in individual portions or 2-serving blocks. Freeze stew base only — add garnish chickpeas, mint, and yogurt fresh at serving. Label with date. Best within 3 months.

Thaw overnight in the fridge, or reheat directly from frozen on stovetop over medium-low with a lid and a splash of water.

---

### Day-by-day plan

**Sunday:** Cook full or double batch. Cool completely before storing. Refrigerate 4 portions, freeze the rest. Store garnish chickpeas separately. Put mint in water in the fridge.

**Monday:** Reheat 1 portion on stovetop. Re-crisp a few garnish chickpeas in a dry skillet. Top with fresh mint and yogurt.

**Tuesday:** Best flavor day — stew has deepened overnight. Same reheat. Add a splash of water if very thick.

**Wednesday:** Reheat last fridge portion. Optionally stir in a handful of fresh chard during last 2 minutes of reheating for more texture.

**Thursday:** Pull a frozen portion and thaw in fridge overnight Wednesday. Reheat Thursday.

**Friday onward:** Frozen portions ready whenever needed over coming weeks.

---

### Storage summary

| Component | Fridge | Freezer |
|---|---|---|
| Stew base | 4–5 days | Up to 3 months |
| Chard (wilted, in stew) | 4–5 days (with stew) | Up to 3 months (with stew) |
| Garnish chickpeas (crispy) | 2–3 days (separate container) | Not recommended |
| Mint | 4–5 days (in water) | Not recommended |
| Yogurt | Per container date | Not recommended |

recipes.name: One-pan Green Fish with Spicy Ginger Oil Soup
recipes.instructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Add the spices and ginger. Cook for 3-4 minutes until the ginger is golden and the spices are beginning to pop.
2. Drain oil into a small bowl and set aside. This is to prevent the ginger from burning in the residual heat.
3. Place the peas, frozen spinach, coriander, green chilli, fish stock and fish sauce into a blender and blend until smooth.
4. Pour into the pan, bring to a gentle simmer, and cook for 5-6 minutes, until thickened.
5. Add the chickpeas to the pan and bring back to a gentle simmer.
6. Once it’s bubbling add the fish to the pan, spoon some sauce over, cover, and cook for 7-8 minutes, until the fish is cooked through. Uncover several times during this period to give a gentle stir and make sure no greens stick to the bottom.
7. Serve in bowls with the sizzled ginger and spices. If desired, you can add some of the spiced oil you previously drained.

NOTE: Vegetable stock (Better Than Bouillon low sodium) -- 1 tsp = 6g -- dissolve 1 tsp in 8oz of water

recipes.name: Pasta with Lentils and Mushrooms
recipes.instructions:
1. Heat oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy pot over medium. Add onion and mushrooms and cook, stirring occasionally, until mushrooms are deeply browned and really starting to stick to bottom of pot, 12–14 minutes. Season lightly with salt.
2. Add garlic, lentils, tomato paste, and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring often, until tomato paste darkens slightly and lentils are coated, about 2 minutes. Add 6 cups water and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer gently until lentils are tender but still have some bite, 10–12 minutes.
3. Add pasta and 1 tsp. salt to pot. Increase heat and bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer, stirring often (especially as the liquid starts to reduce toward the end) and adding very hot water by ½ cupfuls as needed if liquid is reducing too fast, until pasta is cooked through and liquid is reduced, 10–14 minutes.
4. Remove pot from heat and stir in Parmesan, butter, and ½ cup parsley. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
5. Divide among bowls and top with more Parmesan and parsley.

recipes.name: One-Pot Pantry Orzo With Chickpeas (Optimized)
recipes.instructions:
1. Place a rack in middle of oven and preheat to 350°. Heat 3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil in a large Dutch oven or other heavy ovenproof pot over medium. Add 12 garlic cloves, thinly sliced, and cook, stirring often, until softened and fragrant, 2–3 minutes. Add ½ cup pitted green olives, drained and rinsed, coarsely chopped, ¼ cup capers, rinsed, and ¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes and cook, stirring occasionally, just until fragrant, about 2 minutes.
2. Add 12 oz. orzo and one 6-oz. can tomato paste to pot and cook, stirring often, until tomato paste coats orzo and is darkened in color, about 4 minutes. Add one 15.5-oz. can chickpeas, rinsed, 1 tsp. dried thyme, 1 tsp. sugar, ¾ tsp. kosher salt, and 4⅓ cups water, stirring to scrape up any browned bits stuck to bottom of pot.
3. Cover pot, transfer to oven, and bake until orzo is tender and about 80% of liquid has been absorbed, 30–35 minutes (having some liquid on top is okay as the orzo will continue to absorb it). Remove pot from oven, remove lid, and stir in ½ cup Parmesan, finely grated, and 3 tbsp nutritional yeast. Taste orzo and season with more salt if needed and freshly ground black pepper.
4. To serve, divide orzo evenly among shallow bowls. Top each bowl with more Parmesan if desired, then season with a little more black pepper.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

**One-Pot Pantry Orzo With Chickpeas**

This is a genuinely satisfying pantry recipe with a strong nutritional foundation — high fiber, solid protein from Banza orzo + chickpeas, and good fat quality from olive oil. The main issues are sodium (nearly 3x the ideal per-meal target) and total fat, both driven by a small set of identifiable culprits.

---

### Top nutrient contributors

| Nutrient | Primary driver | Secondary driver |
|---|---|---|
| **Calories** | Olive oil (580 kcal) | Banza orzo (1154 kcal) |
| **Fat** | Olive oil (54g) | Green olives (30g) |
| **Saturated fat** | Parmesan (17g) | Olive oil (7.5g) |
| **Sodium** | Green olives (~3,500mg est.) | Capers (~2,000mg est.) + Salt |
| **Protein** | Banza orzo (67g) | Parmesan (23g) + Chickpeas (12.5g) |
| **Fiber** | Banza orzo | Chickpeas |

---

### Sodium

Sodium is the most serious issue here — ~2,826mg per serving is very high. Three ingredients stack on each other: olives, capers, and added salt.

**Green olives → reduce to ½ cup, rinsed thoroughly**
Cutting from 1 cup to ½ cup and rinsing aggressively (soak 10 min, drain, rinse) can reduce sodium by 40–50% from this source alone. Olives are already in the database with accurate data. You lose some brininess but the capers carry that flavor. Texture impact is minimal — you still get the meaty, chewy pops throughout.

**Capers → reduce to ¼ cup, rinsed**
Cutting from ½ cup to ¼ cup and rinsing well. Capers are extremely salty relative to volume. At ¼ cup you still get clear caper presence. Already in the database.

**Salt → reduce to ¾ tsp (from 1½ tsp)**
With olives and capers still present (even reduced), you don't need much added salt. The Parmesan also contributes. Halving it here is conservative — you could go lower. No flavor or texture downside; you can always finish with a pinch at the table.

---

### Fat

**Olive oil → reduce from ⅓ cup to 3 tbsp (¼ cup)**
The original uses a generous amount of oil to bloom the garlic and toast the orzo. 3 tbsp still does the job — garlic softens, orzo gets a light coat for toasting. Savings: ~120 kcal, ~13g fat. Already in the database.

---

### Saturated fat

**Parmesan → reduce to ½ cup (from 1 cup), supplement with nutritional yeast**
Parmesan is the primary sat fat driver. Cutting to ½ cup and adding 3 tbsp nutritional yeast keeps the savory, umami depth without losing it entirely. Nutritional yeast adds a slightly nutty, cheesy flavor, plus B vitamins and some additional protein. Nutritional yeast is already in the database. Texture: slightly less creaminess, but the reduced oil and retained cooking liquid compensate. The finish is still rich.

---

### What stays the same

Everything else — Banza orzo, chickpeas, garlic, tomato paste, thyme, red pepper flakes — is either a strong nutritional contributor or low enough in fat/sodium to leave alone. Banza's protein and fiber are the backbone of what makes this recipe worth eating.

---

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| Nutrient | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 674 kcal | ~560 kcal | −114 kcal |
| Fat | 36.6g | ~25g | −11.6g |
| Saturated fat | 6.8g | ~4.0g | −2.8g |
| Sodium | 2,826mg | ~1,200mg* | −1,626mg |
| Carbs | 64.7g | ~63g | −1.7g |
| Protein | 27.1g | ~26g | −1.1g |
| Fiber | 13.3g | ~13g | −0.3g |

*Sodium estimate is approximate — olives and capers vary significantly by brand and rinsing method.

---

### Summary of changes

| Ingredient | Original | Optimized | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| Olive oil | ⅓ cup | 3 tbsp | Reduce fat/calories |
| Green olives | 1 cup | ½ cup (rinsed) | Primary sodium driver |
| Capers | ½ cup (rinsed) | ¼ cup (rinsed) | Secondary sodium driver |
| Salt | 1½ tsp | ¾ tsp | Redundant with olives/capers/parm |
| Parmesan | 1 cup | ½ cup | Primary sat fat driver |
| Nutritional yeast | — | 3 tbsp (new) | Replaces umami/cheesiness from reduced parm |

recipes.name: NA Lapo Spritzer
recipes.instructions:
2 oz Lapo 
2 oz Grapefruit sparkling water 
1–2 drops Angostura bitters
ABV: 0%

recipes.name: Manhattan
recipes.instructions:
1. Combine: Add the whiskey, sweet vermouth, and 2-3 dashes bitters into a mixing glass.
2. Chill: Fill the mixing glass with ice and stir well for 15–20 seconds until thoroughly chilled.
3. Strain: Strain into a chilled cocktail coupe or martini glass.
4. Garnish: Garnish with a cherry or lemon twist.

recipes.name: Gin & Tonic
recipes.instructions:
1.5oz Bloody Shiraz Gin
3oz Tonic
Lemon

recipes.name: Rice Bowl w/ Impossible Meat (optimized)
recipes.instructions:
### Rice
Rinse 1 cup dry brown rice until water runs clear. Cook according to package directions (typically 2:1 water ratio, ~30 minutes). Fluff and keep warm. For 2 servings aim for about 1.5 cups cooked rice per bowl — if the yield feels generous, hold some back.

### Sauce
Combine soy sauce, miso paste, sesame oil, Toban Djan, gochugaru, grated ginger, and rice vinegar in a small bowl. Whisk until miso is fully dissolved. Taste and adjust — more Toban Djan for depth, more gochugaru for heat. The rice vinegar should be subtle, just a background brightness.

### Protein
Break Impossible patties into rough chunks and cook in a dry or lightly oiled pan over medium-high heat, pressing into the pan to get some color and crust. Cook until browned and cooked through, 4–5 minutes. Set aside.

### Eggs
Soft boil: bring a small pot of water to a full boil, lower eggs in gently, cook exactly 7 minutes, then transfer immediately to an ice bath for 2 minutes. Peel and halve. Yolk should be jammy, not fully set.

### Assemble
Add rice to each bowl. Spoon sauce over and mix lightly to coat. Arrange Impossible meat, egg halves, sliced radish, cucumber, and scallions on top. Serve immediately.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

**Optimized from:** Rice Bowl w/ Impossible Meat (id: 20)

### What changed and why

**Chili crisp → removed**
Primary fat source (~11g fat/serving). Replaced by a layered sauce approach.

**Toban Djan (½ tsp) → added**
Fermented broad bean chili paste. Savory, spicy depth — closest in complexity to what chili crisp was doing, without the oil.

**Gochugaru (½ tsp) → added**
Clean dry heat and red color, no added fat.

**Sesame oil (1 tsp) → added**
Handles mouthfeel and richness that chili crisp oil was providing.

**Miso paste (½ tsp) → added**
Adds fermented roundness to the sauce. Fully dissolve before adding to rice.

**Ginger (1 tsp, fresh grated) → added**
Brightness, anti-inflammatory. Goes into the sauce.

**Rice vinegar (½ tsp) → added**
Particularly useful in rice bowls — lifts the sauce, cuts through the richness of Impossible meat, keeps the rice from feeling heavy. More important here than in the noodle bowl version.

**Radish → reduced from 60g to 30g**
Balanced against cucumber addition.

**Cucumber (40g) → added**
Crunch and freshness. Classic in rice bowl formats.

**Impossible meat → kept at 2 patties**
Household includes a non-meat eater. Sat fat is largely from this ingredient and was accepted as a trade-off.

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| Nutrient | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 763 kcal | ~675 kcal | −88 kcal |
| Fat | 30.6g | ~20g | −10.6g |
| Saturated Fat | 10g | ~8.8g | −1.2g |
| Sodium | 1197mg | ~950mg | −247mg |
| Protein | 33.4g | ~33.4g | — |
| Carbs | 88g | 88g | — |
| Fiber | 9.2g | ~9.5g | +0.3g |

recipes.name: Rice Bowl w/ Shrimp (optimized)
recipes.instructions:
### Rice
Rinse 1 cup dry brown rice until water runs clear. Cook according to package directions (typically 2:1 water ratio, ~30 minutes). Fluff and keep warm. Aim for about 1.5 cups cooked rice per bowl.

### Sauce
Combine soy sauce, miso paste, sesame oil, Toban Djan, gochugaru, grated ginger, and rice vinegar in a small bowl. Whisk until miso is fully dissolved. Taste and adjust — more Toban Djan for depth, more gochugaru for heat. The rice vinegar should be subtle, just a background brightness.

### Shrimp
Use about 8–10 medium shrimp (200g total), peeled and deveined — about 4–5 per person. Pat dry. Cook in a lightly oiled pan over medium-high heat, 1–2 minutes per side until just pink and opaque. Don't overcook, they go rubbery fast. Add a pinch of gochugaru in the pan for extra color if you like.

### Eggs
Soft boil: bring a small pot of water to a full boil, lower eggs in gently, cook exactly 7 minutes, then transfer immediately to an ice bath for 2 minutes. Peel and halve. Yolk should be jammy, not fully set.

### Assemble
Add rice to each bowl. Spoon sauce over and mix lightly to coat. Arrange shrimp, egg halves, sliced radish, cucumber, and scallions on top. Serve immediately.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

**Optimized from:** Rice Bowl w/ Impossible Meat (id: 20) → shrimp variant

### What changed and why

**Impossible meat → replaced with Shrimp (200g)**
Shrimp is dramatically leaner than Impossible — very low fat, zero saturated fat, high protein. 200g total (8–10 medium shrimp) yields about 4–5 per person, comparable protein to the Impossible version with a fraction of the fat. The bowl goes from a rich, meaty character to something lighter and cleaner.

**Chili crisp → removed**
Same reasoning as the Impossible optimized version — oil is the primary fat culprit.

**Toban Djan (½ tsp) → added**
Savory fermented depth in the sauce.

**Gochugaru (½ tsp) → added**
Clean heat, no oil.

**Sesame oil (1 tsp) → added**
Mouthfeel and richness without the volume of chili crisp oil.

**Miso paste (½ tsp) → added**
Fermented roundness in the sauce.

**Ginger (1 tsp, fresh grated) → added**
Particularly good with shrimp — classic pairing. Brightness and warmth.

**Rice vinegar (½ tsp) → added**
Lifts the sauce, especially important here since shrimp is lighter than Impossible and the bowl could otherwise feel underseasoned.

**Radish → reduced from 60g to 30g; Cucumber (40g) → added**
Same as Impossible optimized version.

### Nutrition comparison (per serving, estimated)

| Nutrient | Original (Impossible) | Shrimp Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 763 kcal | ~570 kcal | −193 kcal |
| Fat | 30.6g | ~10g | −20.6g |
| Saturated Fat | 10g | ~2g | −8g |
| Sodium | 1197mg | ~750mg | −447mg |
| Protein | 33.4g | ~36g | +2.6g |
| Carbs | 88g | 88g | — |
| Fiber | 9.2g | ~9.5g | +0.3g |

The shrimp version is the leanest of the four bowl variants by a significant margin.

ingredients.notes: peeled and deveined, approx 8-10 medium shrimp

recipes.name: Noodle Bowl w/ Impossible Meat (optimized)
recipes.instructions:
### Sauce
Combine soy sauce, miso paste, sesame oil, Toban Djan, gochugaru, grated ginger, and rice vinegar in a small bowl. Whisk together until the miso is fully dissolved. Set aside. This is the flavor base for the whole bowl — taste it and adjust: more Toban Djan for depth, more gochugaru for heat. The rice vinegar should be barely perceptible — just background brightness.

### Noodles
Cook ramen bricks in boiling unsalted water according to package directions, erring slightly undercooked — about 30 seconds less than indicated. They'll finish absorbing sauce in the bowl. Drain and rinse briefly with cold water to stop cooking and prevent clumping.

### Protein
Break Impossible patties into rough chunks and cook in a dry or lightly oiled pan over medium-high heat, pressing into the pan to get some color and crust. Cook until browned and cooked through, 4–5 minutes. Set aside.

### Eggs
Soft boil: bring a small pot of water to a full boil, lower eggs in gently, cook exactly 7 minutes, then transfer immediately to an ice bath for 2 minutes. Peel and halve. Yolk should be jammy, not fully set.

### Assemble
Add drained noodles to each bowl. Pour sauce over and toss to coat — use tongs or chopsticks to work it through. Arrange Impossible meat, egg halves, sliced radish, cucumber, and scallions on top. Serve immediately.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

**Optimized from:** Noodle Bowl w/ Impossible Meat (id: 21)

### What changed and why

**Chili crisp → removed**
The oil in chili crisp was the primary fat contributor (~11g fat per serving). Heat and complexity are now handled by Toban Djan and Gochugaru instead.

**Toban Djan (½ tsp) → added**
Fermented broad bean chili paste. Replaces the savory, umami depth that chili crisp provided. Low calorie, minimal fat. A small amount goes a long way.

**Gochugaru (½ tsp) → added**
Dry Korean chili flake. Provides clean heat and red color without added oil. Bloom briefly in the pan for best flavor.

**Sesame oil (1 tsp) → added**
Handles the mouthfeel and noodle coating that chili crisp's oil was previously doing.

**Miso paste (½ tsp) → added**
Stirred into the sauce (with soy sauce + sesame oil) to add fermented depth and roundness. Adds minimal sodium at this quantity.

**Ginger (1 tsp, fresh grated) → added**
Anti-inflammatory, aids digestion, adds brightness. Bloom with the Impossible meat or stir into the sauce.

**Radish → reduced from 60g to 30g**
Slightly reduced to make room for cucumber without overloading the garnish layer.

**Cucumber (40g) → added**
Adds crunch, water content, and freshness. Makes the bowl feel lighter and more balanced — common in cold or room-temp noodle bowl formats.

**Impossible meat → kept at 2 patties**
Household includes a non-meat eater. Sat fat (~7.8g/serving) is largely from this ingredient and was accepted as a trade-off.

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| Nutrient | Original | Optimized | Change |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 788 kcal | 708 kcal | −80 kcal |
| Fat | 32.6g | 24.9g | −7.7g |
| Saturated Fat | 9.5g | 7.8g | −1.7g |
| Sodium | 1193mg | 1146mg | −47mg |
| Protein | 38.4g | 38.5g | — |
| Carbs | 52.9g | 48.2g | −4.7g |
| Fiber | 11.7g | 11.6g | — |

Combine soy sauce, miso paste, sesame oil, Toban Djan, gochugaru, grated ginger, and rice vinegar in a small bowl. Whisk together until the miso is fully dissolved. Set aside. Taste and adjust — more Toban Djan for depth, more gochugaru for heat. The rice vinegar should be barely perceptible — just background brightness.

### Noodles
Cook ramen bricks in boiling unsalted water according to package directions, erring slightly undercooked — about 30 seconds less than indicated. Drain and rinse briefly with cold water to stop cooking and prevent clumping.

### Shrimp
Use about 8–10 medium shrimp (200g total), peeled and deveined — about 4–5 per person. Pat dry. Cook in a lightly oiled pan over medium-high heat, 1–2 minutes per side until just pink and opaque. Don't overcook, they go rubbery fast. Add a pinch of gochugaru in the pan for extra color if you like.

### Eggs

recipes.name: Black Bean Avocado Brownies (Optimized)
recipes.instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350F. Grease and line 8x8 pan.
2. Place all ingredients besides chocolate chips into a food processor and blend til smooth. This batter needs to be thick.
3. Add in chocolate chips and fold into batter.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan. Add in extra chips, nuts, or peanut butter swirl (optional)
5. Bake for 25-30min. Let cool before cutting.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

### Top nutrient contributors
- **Calories:** Maple syrup (440 kcal) and chocolate chips (233 kcal) account for ~70% of total recipe calories
- **Protein:** Black beans (13.5g) and eggs (12g) are the workhorses; PB Powder adds 9g
- **Fiber:** Black beans, avocado, and cocoa powder
- **Fat:** Avocado (monounsaturated) and olive oil

### Changes from original

| Change | Original | Optimized | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| Maple syrup | 120g | 80g | Biggest calorie source; monkfruit covers sweetness gap |
| Monkfruit | 1/16 tsp | 1/4 tsp | Compensates for reduced maple syrup |
| Olive oil | 1 Tbsp | removed | No flavor payoff in brownies |
| Almond butter | — | 1 Tbsp | Adds protein, nuttiness, and better binding |
| PB Powder | 16g | 24g | More protein, deeper chocolate-peanut flavor |
| Espresso powder | — | 1/4 tsp | Amplifies chocolate richness without coffee taste |

### Nutrition comparison (per serving)

| Metric | Original | Optimized | Δ |
|---|---|---|---|
| Calories | 94.6 kcal | ~87 kcal | −7.6 |
| Fat | 3.74g | ~3.9g | +0.2 |
| Sat fat | 1.13g | ~1.13g | — |
| Carbs | 12.7g | ~11.0g | −1.7 |
| Sugar | 7.31g | ~5.6g | −1.7 |
| Protein | 3.28g | ~3.7g | +0.4 |
| Fiber | 2.86g | ~2.86g | — |
| Sodium | 61.9mg | ~61.9mg | — |

recipes.name: Black Bean Cherry Avocado Brownies (Optimized)
recipes.instructions:
1. Cook down cherries with lemon juice until reduced and jammy.
2. Preheat oven to 350F. Grease and line 8x8 pan.
3. Place all ingredients besides chocolate chips and cherries into a food processor and blend til smooth. This batter needs to be thick.
4. Add in chocolate chips and cherries and fold into batter.
5. Pour batter into prepared pan. Add in extra chips, nuts, or peanut butter swirl (optional)
6. Bake for 25-30min. Let cool before cutting.
recipes.optimizeAnalysis:
## Optimization Notes

### Top nutrient contributors
- **Calories:** Maple syrup (440 kcal) and chocolate chips (233 kcal) account for ~70% of total recipe calories
- **Protein:** Black beans (13.5g) and eggs (12g); PB Powder omitted to preserve cherry/lemon profile
- **Fiber:** Black beans, frozen cherries, avocado, and cocoa powder (56g — higher than base recipe)
- **Fat:** Avocado and olive oil

### Changes from original

| Change | Original | Optimized | Reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| Maple syrup | 120g | 80g | Biggest calorie source; monkfruit covers sweetness gap |
| Monkfruit | 1/16 tsp | 1/4 tsp | Compensates for reduced maple syrup |
| Olive oil | 1 Tbsp | removed | No flavor payoff in brownies |
| Almond butter | — | 1 Tbsp | Adds protein, nuttiness, and better binding |
| Espresso powder | — | 1/4 tsp | Amplifies chocolate and gives the cherry a foil |

*PB Powder not added — cherry/lemon profile doesn't benefit from it.*
